perturbation.original_text: (6) The payment of the Advertising Cost Compensation shall occur monthly insofar as the sum due exceeds a value of 100 EUR / 100 USD. If this is not the case, the sum due can be retained until a total value of at least 100 EUR / 100 USD is reached, at the latest, upon termination of the Affiliate's participation in the Program.
perturbation.changed_text: (6) The payment of the Advertising Cost Compensation shall occur monthly insofar as the sum due exceeds a value of 100 EUR / 100 USD. If this is not the case, the sum due can be retained until a total value of at least 100 EUR / 100 USD is reached, at the latest, upon the earlier of (a) six months from the date the first commission was earned or (b) termination of the Affiliate's participation in the Program.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that if the Advertising Cost Compensation does not exceed 100 EUR/USD monthly, the sum can be retained until it reaches at least 100 EUR/USD, or upon termination of the program. The modified text adds a six-month limit. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the affiliate might expect the payment to be retained until termination, but now the payment will occur after 6 months regardless if it reached the threshold or not, which is contradictory to the original agreement that it is 'at the latest, upon termination of the Affiliate's participation in the Program.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: (6) The payment of the Advertising Cost Compensation shall occur monthly insofar as the sum due exceeds a value of 100 EUR / 100 USD. If this is not the case, the sum due can be retained until a total value of at least 100 EUR / 100 USD is reached, at the latest, upon the earlier of (a) six months from the date the first commission was earned or (b) termination of the Affiliate's participation in the Program.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a six-month limit on retaining Advertising Cost Compensation, even if the 100 EUR/USD threshold isn't met. This directly contradicts the original provision that allows retention 'at the latest, upon termination of the Affiliate's participation in the Program,' creating a clear conflict regarding when the accumulated compensation must be paid. The affiliate might reasonably expect the compensation to accumulate until termination, but the six-month limit overrides this expectation.

perturbation.original_text: WHITHIN 14 DAYS OF THE ANNOUNCEMENT OF CHANGED PARTICIPATION TERMS, AFFILIATE AND SOFTWARE PUBLISHER MAY OBJECT TO SUCH CHANGES. IF NO OBJECTION IS RECEIVED DURING THIS TIME, THE PARTICIPANT IS DEEMED TO HAVE ACCEPTED THE CHANGES AND THEY WILL BECOME PART OF THE EXISTING RELATIONS UNDER THE AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: WHITHIN 14 DAYS OF THE ANNOUNCEMENT OF CHANGED PARTICIPATION TERMS, AFFILIATE AND SOFTWARE PUBLISHER MAY OBJECT TO SUCH CHANGES. IF NO OBJECTION IS RECEIVED DURING THIS TIME, THE PARTICIPANT IS DEEMED TO HAVE ACCEPTED THE CHANGES AND THEY WILL BECOME PART OF THE EXISTING RELATIONS UNDER THE AGREEMENT. Notwithstanding the foregoing, element 5 reserves the right to implement any changes immediately if deemed necessary for legal or security reasons.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that changes to the terms require a 14-day objection period before being implemented. The added sentence allows element 5 to bypass this objection period and implement changes immediately for legal or security reasons. This is an in-text contradiction because it creates uncertainty as to when and how changes will be implemented, invalidating the original 14 day notice requirement and creating confusion regarding contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: element 5 RESERVES THE RIGHT TO MODIFY AND TO SUPPLEMENT THESE GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS AT ANY TIME. THE CURRENT VERSION WILL BE MAINTAINED FOR VIEWING AS A HYPERLINK ON element 5's WEB SITE IN THE CONTROL PANEL. MOREOVER, element 5 WILL ANNOUNNOUNCE ANY CHANGES TO THESE TERMS VIA E- MAIL. WHITHIN 14 DAYS OF THE ANNOUNCEMENT OF CHANGED PARTICIPATION TERMS, AFFILIATE AND SOFTWARE PUBLISHER MAY OBJECT TO SUCH CHANGES. IF NO OBJECTION IS RECEIVED DURING THIS TIME, THE PARTICIPANT IS DEEMED TO HAVE ACCEPTED THE CHANGES AND THEY WILL BECOME PART OF THE EXISTING RELATIONS UNDER THE AGREEMENT. Notwithstanding the foregoing, element 5 reserves the right to implement any changes immediately if deemed necessary for legal or security reasons.
perturbation.justification: The added sentence in the Changed Text, 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, element 5 reserves the right to implement any changes immediately if deemed necessary for legal or security reasons,' directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which states a 14-day objection period for changes. This creates an inconsistency as the 14-day notice requirement is nullified for legal or security reasons, leading to uncertainty about when changes can be implemented. This is an Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may cancel this Agreement at any time, with or without supplying a reason, through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may cancel this Agreement at any time, with or without supplying a reason, through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel. However, if the Affiliate terminates the agreement within the first six months, element 5 reserves the right to withhold any unpaid Advertising Cost Compensation.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants either party the right to cancel the agreement at any time without specifying any restrictions. The added clause introduces a penalty for the Affiliate if they terminate the agreement within the first six months, by allowing element 5 to withhold any unpaid advertising cost compensation. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the original agreement grants a free pass to end the contract when ever, which is no longer valid given the 6 month restriction, making the parties unsure of their obligations in case of termination before the sixth month.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Agreement period / Cancellation The term of this Agreement will begin upon acceptance of Affiliate's Program application and will end when terminated by either party of this Agreement. This Agreement is entered into for an unlimited period of time. Either party may cancel this Agreement at any time, with or without supplying a reason, through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel. However, if the Affiliate terminates the agreement within the first six months, element 5 reserves the right to withhold any unpaid Advertising Cost Compensation.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that either party could cancel the agreement at any time without any restrictions. The changed text adds a restriction: If the affiliate cancels within the first six months, they may have unpaid advertising cost compensation withheld. This creates a direct conflict with the original agreement, as the right to cancel 'at any time' is now conditional, leading to a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this case, as the change introduces the contradiction.
